span.p: A breaking change you must roll out

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Can old + new behavior coexist behind one surface for a transition window?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Expand: ship the NEW field/endpoint/version alongside the old. Both satisfiable at once.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Dual-serve: old clients use the old path, new clients adopt the new. No one is forced yet.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Announce on a clock: Deprecation header on the old + Sunset date + changelog + portal notice

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Are there still callers on the old path as the Sunset date nears?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Extend the window or reach out to named laggards; never silently retire live traffic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Contract: remove the old field/endpoint/version after the Sunset date

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: New major version /v2 in parallel; run the same announce -> measure -> contract clock on /v1
